title: Send The Left Tipper Tote To Hospital From The VISU_DATA Screen
summary: Use the SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL control on the Operator Station HMI VISU_DATA screen to send the tote currently at the left tipper to the hospital.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to route the tote currently at the left tipper to the hospital using the documented VISU_DATA screen control.
not_for: Do not use for sending a tote from the right tipper to the hospital.
Do not use when the tote is not currently at the left tipper.
Do not use as a recovery procedure for AGV stuck or communication fault conditions; those are described in other source sections, not this procedure.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the Operator Station HMI, navigate to the VISU_DATA screen, identify the SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button, and press it for the tote currently at the left tipper.

safety_notes: This runbook is limited to the documented HMI action on the VISU_DATA screen.
The source does not describe confirmation prompts, interlocks, or post-action confirmation indicators for this control.
Do not assume additional system behavior beyond the source-stated function of the button.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: VISU_DATA screen is available on the Operator Station HMI.
A tote is currently at the left tipper if the action is to apply as documented.
external_references: VISU_DATA screen overview context record: ctx_manual_visu_data_screen_overview_v1

visual_references.description: Operator Station HMI VISU_DATA screen showing login controls, message banner, and tipper statistics.
Operator station display controls showing how to navigate to the VISU_DATA screen, including F5 mapping.
Additional VISU_DATA screen reference showing operator stats on the data screen.

steps.title: Open the VISU_DATA screen
steps.purpose: Get to the documented HMI screen that contains the hospital-routing control.
steps.instruction: At the operator station HMI, open the VISU_DATA screen. The source indicates this screen can be reached through the operator station display controls and screen navigation.
steps.expected_result: The VISU_DATA screen is displayed on the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can view the VISU_DATA screen with its standard data-screen elements such as login/logout controls, message banner, and tipper-related information.
steps.failure_condition: The VISU_DATA screen cannot be reached or the operator is not on the correct HMI screen.

actions.label: Open VISU_DATA
actions.exact_action: Open the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall VISU_DATA screen layout including login controls, message banner, and tipper statistics.
Display controls and screen-navigation mapping showing access to Visu_Data.

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator station HMI is unavailable.
The VISU_DATA screen cannot be accessed from the operator station controls.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The listed control mapping includes F5 to "Visu_Data."

Figure 4-20 and Table 4-6 describe the Operator Station HMI Data Screen, including the informational banner, logged-in user ID, LOGIN/LOGOUT buttons, Tipper Stats, Process Status, RunTime Meters, screen selection drop-down, hospital send buttons, and PAUSE TIPPER.

steps.title: Locate the SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button
steps.purpose: Identify the specific control used to send the left-tipper tote to the hospital.
steps.instruction: On the VISU_DATA screen, locate the SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button.
steps.expected_result: The SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button is visible on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can clearly identify the SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL control among the VISU_DATA screen controls.
steps.failure_condition: The SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button is not visible or cannot be distinguished on the VISU_DATA screen.

actions.label: Find hospital-send control
actions.exact_action: Locate the SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_DATA screen area containing hospital-routing controls, including SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL.

stop_or_escalate_if: The SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button is not present on the VISU_DATA screen.
The displayed screen does not match the documented VISU_DATA screen overview.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 4-20 and Table 4-6 describe the Operator Station HMI Data Screen, including ... hospital send buttons ...
Table 4-6 continues with Process Status, RunTime Meters, direct screen selection, SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL, SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL, and PAUSE TIPPER.

steps.title: Confirm the action applies to the left tipper tote
steps.purpose: Ensure the operator uses the control only for the tote identified by the source as eligible for this action.
steps.instruction: Confirm that the tote to be sent is the tote currently at the left tipper, because the documented function of this button applies to that tote.
steps.expected_result: The operator has confirmed the action is intended for the tote currently at the left tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote to be routed matches the source-defined scope of the SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button.
steps.failure_condition: The tote is not currently at the left tipper or the operator cannot confirm that it is.

actions.label: Confirm left-tipper applicability
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the action applies to the tote currently at the left tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_DATA screen context while confirming the action is being applied from the left-tipper side.

stop_or_escalate_if: It is unclear whether the tote is currently at the left tipper.
The operator intends to route a tote from the right tipper instead.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button will send the tote currently at the left tipper to the hospital.

steps.title: Press SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL
steps.purpose: Initiate the documented hospital-send action for the tote currently at the left tipper.
steps.instruction: Press the SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.expected_result: The documented hospital-send action is initiated for the tote currently at the left tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: The button press is accepted and the system initiates the left-tipper hospital-send action as documented.
steps.failure_condition: The button does not initiate the documented hospital-send action.

actions.label: Send left tote to hospital
actions.exact_action: Press the SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL control on the VISU_DATA screen before and during button press.

stop_or_escalate_if: The button does not initiate the documented hospital-send action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button will send the tote currently at the left tipper to the hospital.

steps.title: Verify the tote was sent if an indicator is available
steps.purpose: Perform source-limited verification without assuming undocumented prompts or indicators.
steps.instruction: Verify on the HMI or through system observation that the tote at the left tipper has been sent to the hospital, but only if that result is visible from available source-backed indicators.
steps.expected_result: The operator observes source-supported indication or system behavior consistent with the tote being sent to the hospital, if such indication is available.
steps.healthy_condition: Available HMI or observable system behavior is consistent with the left-tipper tote having been sent to the hospital.
steps.failure_condition: No observable result is available, or available observation suggests the tote was not sent to the hospital.

actions.label: Check for visible result
actions.exact_action: Verify on the HMI or through system observation that the left-tipper tote has been sent to the hospital, if that result is visible from available source-backed indicators.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_DATA screen elements such as tipper-related information or message banner that may provide visible indication, if present.

stop_or_escalate_if: The source provides no confirmation prompt or follow-up indicator and the operator cannot verify the result.
Observed behavior suggests the tote was not sent to the hospital.
The button press did not appear to initiate the documented action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The VISU_DATA screen includes an informational banner for alarms and informational messages and tipper statistics.
The SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button will send the tote currently at the left tipper to the hospital.
The source does not describe confirmation prompts or follow-up indicators for this action.

success_criteria: The SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button is accessed from the VISU_DATA screen.
The action is applied to the tote currently at the left tipper.
The documented hospital-send action is initiated for that tote.
If visible source-backed indicators are available, they are consistent with the tote being sent to the hospital.
healthy_conditions: VISU_DATA screen is accessible on the operator station HMI.
The SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button is present on the VISU_DATA screen.
The tote to be acted on is currently at the left tipper.
Available HMI or observable system behavior is consistent with the tote being sent to the hospital.
failure_conditions: SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button is not visible or cannot be identified.
The tote is not currently at the left tipper.
Pressing the button does not initiate the documented hospital-send action.
The source does not provide enough confirmation detail to verify completion from the HMI.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button does not initiate the documented hospital-send action.
Escalate if the operator cannot access the VISU_DATA screen or cannot locate the SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button.
Escalate if the result cannot be verified and operational confirmation is required, because the source does not describe confirmation prompts or follow-up indicators for this action.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Navigation controls used to reach VISU_DATA.
Additional VISU_DATA screen reference for screen recognition.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL button will send the tote currently at the left tipper to the hospital.

Figure 4-20 and Table 4-6 describe the Operator Station HMI Data Screen, including the informational banner, logged-in user ID, LOGIN/LOGOUT buttons, Tipper Stats, Process Status, RunTime Meters, screen selection drop-down, hospital send buttons, and PAUSE TIPPER.
Table 4-6 continues with Process Status, RunTime Meters, direct screen selection, SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL, SEND RIGHT TO HOSPITAL, and PAUSE TIPPER.

missing_details: The source does not provide a documented confirmation prompt for this action.
The source does not provide explicit post-action indicators to verify completion.
The source does not state whether login is required before using SEND LEFT TO HOSPITAL.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required for this action.
